pages.name: 01-dashboard
good: Calorie ring with centered KCAL value creates strong focal point and clear hierarchy
Green brand color used purposefully for remaining calories and streak elements
Water intake section with circular progress and quick-add buttons is highly functional
Weight progress chart with goal line dashed in green is contextually informative
Card-based layout with consistent bg-gray-900 creates clean sectioning
bad: BMI (19.8) and day streak (0) are displayed at same visual weight as primary calorie data — no hierarchy differentiation
Right panel with BMI and streak feels cramped and under-spaced compared to the dominant calorie card
Water intake quick-add buttons (150ml, 250ml, 500ml, 750ml) have identical styling with no visual differentiation for most common action
'Next milestone' card uses very similar dark green background that barely separates it from other cards — low contrast
Macros section shows all zeros with no skeleton/placeholder state — feels abandoned
Weight chart y-axis labels (57-61) are extremely small and low contrast, barely legible
improvements: Increase BMI value to text-2xl and add a colored pill badge (green for healthy) directly on the stat instead of plain text below
Add a subtle divider or increase padding between calorie ring card and BMI/streak card to reduce cramped feel — target min 16px gap
Highlight the 250ml Glass button with a slightly brighter border or scale-up to indicate most common serving
Increase weight chart label font size to at least 11px and use text-gray-400 for better legibility
Add a faint progress indicator or 'Log your first meal' micro-CTA inside the macros zeros to reduce empty feeling

pages.name: 02-tracker-today
good: Quick Add section with Indian meal presets (Dal Tadka, Roti, Idli) is an excellent localization touch and solves the blank-state problem proactively
Date navigator with back/forward arrows and TODAY badge is clean and highly usable
Macro breakdown (Protein/Carbs/Fat) with both current and goal values in the bottom summary bar is information-dense but readable
Empty state illustration with salad bowl and + icon plus CTA button is polished and encouraging
Search bar with contextual placeholder text (e.g. Dal Tadka, Roti, Rice...) reinforces Indian food context
bad: Quick Add cards have inconsistent emoji sizes relative to card size — some emojis appear small and misaligned vertically
Calorie count under Quick Add items (180 kcal, 70 kcal) uses same text-gray-400 as labels — calorie number should be more prominent
The nutrition totals bar at bottom shows 0g / 150g but the progress bars are all flat gray — no visual differentiation between protein/carbs/fat bar colors
Settings gear icon in top right has no tooltip or label — unclear what it controls
2000 kcal goal in the tracker differs from 2454 kcal shown in the dashboard — inconsistency creates confusion
improvements: Color-code the three macro progress bars: blue for protein, yellow/amber for carbs, orange for fat — matching dashboard macro colors
Make kcal values in Quick Add cards text-white font-semibold instead of gray to create secondary hierarchy within the card
Align calorie display: ensure food log page uses the same 2454 kcal goal as the dashboard, not 2000 kcal
Add a tooltip or label on the gear icon: 'Log Settings' — or replace with a more contextual icon like a filter
Add a meal-type segmentation (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner / Snacks) above the empty state to give users structure before they log

pages.name: 05-profile
good: Stats badge row (BMI, Maintenance, Your Goal, Protein, Carbs, Fat) provides an at-a-glance summary — the green highlight on Your Goal badge is effective
Identity card with avatar initial, age, gender, height is clean and well-structured
BMI 19.8 — Healthy weight green inline label adds positive reinforcement
Form layout for Update Goals with clear field labels is functional and unambiguous
Dev Mode Active banner is clearly differentiated with green/red text — good developer UX
bad: Avatar is just a letter 'J' in a circle — no option to add photo creates a generic feel that reduces personal connection
Stats badges have no units on some values (184g Protein reads oddly — should clarify it is a daily target)
The goals form (Current Weight, Goal Weight, Timeline, Activity Level, Diet Type) is vertically long with no visual grouping or section labels between fields
Sign Out button uses red outline style but is positioned below Account section with insufficient separation from Re-do Onboarding — they could be accidentally confused
Dropdowns (Timeline, Activity Level, Diet Type) use default browser styling — inconsistent with the custom dark theme
improvements: Add a camera icon overlay on the avatar circle to hint at photo upload capability, even if future feature — increases perceived completeness
Group goals form fields into two visual sections: 'Weight Goals' (current/goal weight) and 'Lifestyle' (activity, diet, timeline) with small section headers in text-xs text-gray-500 uppercase
Replace native select dropdowns with custom-styled dark dropdowns matching bg-gray-800 border-gray-700 to maintain design consistency
Add subtitle text under each stat badge: e.g. 'Daily Target' under Protein/Carbs/Fat to clarify context
Increase vertical spacing between the Re-do Onboarding list item and Sign Out button to at least 24px, and consider moving Sign Out to a destructive zone at the very bottom with a separator

pages.name: 08-dishes-empty
good: Logged 5 times counter on the dish card provides useful engagement data
Veg/non-veg tag badge in green is a culturally relevant and visually clear affordance for Indian users
Macro breakdown (P/C/F) inline on the dish card is compact and informative
Add another dish dashed-border button is a clean affordance that implies the list pattern
bad: The page title says 'empty state (no dishes created yet)' but the screenshot actually shows one dish (meal 1) — the empty state design itself is not visible, making evaluation harder; the 'empty' concept is not communicated with an illustration or zero-state message
Edit and delete icons on the dish card are very small (approximately 16px) and closely spaced — touch targets too small for mobile, high misclick risk
The helper text 'Custom dishes appear in food search when you log meals' is low contrast and positioned below the fold — most users will miss this key piece of information
Dish card shows '202 kcal /100g' but the base quantity notation is unclear — users may not understand if this is per serving or per 100g
No search or filter functionality visible — becomes a usability problem as dish count grows
improvements: Increase edit/delete icon touch targets to minimum 44x44px by wrapping in a larger clickable area with p-3 padding
Move the 'Custom dishes appear in food search' helper text to a highlighted info card at the top of the page with an info icon — treat it as onboarding context
Clarify nutrition display: change '202 kcal /100g' to '202 kcal per serving (300g)' to remove ambiguity about the base unit
Add a search bar at the top of My Dishes page above the dish list to support future scalability
For true empty state: add a chef illustration or icon with heading 'No dishes yet' and subtext 'Create your first custom recipe' above the New Dish CTA

pages.name: 09-dishes-create
good: Green border focus state on the dish name input is clear and on-brand
Two-step mental model (name first, then search ingredients) is logical and reduces cognitive load
Chef hat icon placeholder in ingredients area is a nice empty-state micro-detail
Cancel/Save Dish button pair with contrasting styles (gray vs green) clearly communicates the primary action
Placeholder text examples (e.g. My Poha, Mom's Dal, Evening Smoothie) are culturally resonant and helpful
bad: Form is a floating card but has no visible modal overlay/backdrop — feels disconnected from the page context without a dimmed background
Ingredients section label 'INGREDIENTS — search and add' uses a long inline label that would be better as a label + subtitle separated
No diet type selector (Veg/Non-Veg) in the create form — but the dish list shows a veg badge, meaning this data is required but not collected here
Save Dish button is active even with empty fields — should be disabled (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) until both name and at least one ingredient are filled
No character counter on dish name field — users don't know length limits, risk creating truncated names
improvements: Add a Veg / Non-Veg toggle (pill toggle style, green for veg, orange for non-veg) below the dish name input to collect diet type during creation
Disable the Save Dish button until dish name is non-empty and at least one ingredient is added — use opacity-40 and cursor-not-allowed states
Add a semi-transparent dark backdrop (bg-black/60) behind the create card if it is a modal, or restructure as a full page with a back button for clarity
Split ingredient label into: label text 'INGREDIENTS' on its own line, then a softer subtitle 'Search and add foods below' in text-gray-400 text-sm
Add a serving size / total portions input field below the ingredients list so nutrition can be calculated per serving rather than just total

top_fixes: Unify calorie goal across all pages — the Food Log shows 2000 kcal while Dashboard shows 2454 kcal; this inconsistency destroys user trust and must be a single source of truth from the profile goal calculation

Color-code all macro bars consistently across Dashboard and Food Tracker (blue=protein, amber=carbs, orange=fat) — currently macros are indistinguishable gray bars that convey no information at a glance

Replace all native browser select dropdowns (Profile goals form, Diet Type) with custom dark-themed dropdowns matching bg-gray-800 border border-gray-700 rounded-lg to close the most obvious design inconsistency in the app

Add a Veg/Non-Veg toggle to the dish creation form and increase edit/delete touch targets on dish cards to 44px minimum — both are critical UX gaps that affect daily usability for Indian users who filter by diet type

Disable Save/submit buttons until required fields are filled across all forms (dish creation, food log) and add micro-validation feedback — currently all CTAs are always active which leads to empty submissions and poor error states
